# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:  
# Parameter file containing default parameter values in Azure Resource Manager templates
parameters:   
 # List of objects that contain the parameters and their respective defaults, referencing another resource id or name  here by its full path as a string. Referenced object types include ARM resources within your template itself (like Controllers), built-ins data structures such as arrays etc., any parameter value set at instance level for this deployment too
 - keyVaultName    : '{your_keyvaultname}'   # Name of the Key Vault resource  [Required]     , Default: ''     
 - secretNames    : ['secret1', 'secret2']        # List or Array contain names all secrets will be created on Azure Kusto . This list can have more than one item if required. e.g., array = {'firstSecret','secondSecret'} [Required]  , Default: []  
 - applicationObjectIds    : ['obj1', 'obj2']        # List or Array of objects which are the ids for applications that will access these secrets . This list can have more than one item if required. e.g., array = {'firstObj','secondApp'} [Required]  , Default: []
 - servicePrincipalObjectIds    : ['obj1', 'obj2']        # List or Array of objects which are the ids for services that will access these secrets . This list can have more than one item if required. e.g., array = {'firstObj','secondService'} [Required]  , Default: []
 - location    : resources.[location].name     # The Azure Location (i.e. West US, North Europe etc.) for your Resource group or the specific resource region you want to deploy in . This value can be changed if needed but it needs a valid ARM template deployment context   [Required]  , Default: ''   
 - accessTier : 'Bing的热'        # The Access Tiers provide different levels of performance and availability for your data. Choices include Hot, Cool (Default) or Archive       . This value can be changed if needed but it needs a valid ARM template deployment context  , Default: ''  
 - enabled    : true          # If you want to enable encryption on the secrets [Optional]     , default is false      , Possible values True/False. It must only contain either 'True' or not include this property at all . ie., cannot be included and excluded by user input ，Default: False
